summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the repository root containing GYP command-substitution syntax (`<!(...)`) in its sources/targets configuration. npm implicitly invokes `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present — even with no declared install/postinstall script — and the GYP configure step evaluates `<!(...)` as a shell command. This causes the embedded command to execute on every `npm install`, functionally identical to a lifecycle hook. The package presents itself as a Backstage GitLab plugin (a pure TypeScript/React plugin family that has no legitimate need for a native addon or a binding.gyp), and the binding.gyp is the only mechanism by which install-time code execution is introduced. The combination of (a) a binding.gyp in a package whose declared purpose is a Backstage frontend plugin, and (b) command-expansion syntax embedded in that binding.gyp, is the documented install-time-RCE fingerprint for this delivery vector.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
